title: Gujarat ATS arrests 5 more in Jaish module; probe reveals 8 IED trial runs
content: The Gujarat ATS has apprehended five additional suspects connected to a Jaish-e-Mohammed module, uncovered while they were preparing for explosive device attacks. This group had a history of recruitment and radicalization, engaging in bomb-making training for several years. Investigators reported that they executed at least eight trial runs for explosive timers, and the ongoing investigation aims to unveil potential targets and their external affiliations.

title: West Indies cricket legend Garry Sobers dies at 89
content: Cricket icon Garfield Sobers has passed away at the age of 89. He represented the West Indies in 93 Test matches during his career. Sobers scored 8,032 runs and took 235 wickets in Tests. He famously scored 365 runs in a single Test innings. Sobers also achieved six sixes in one over in first-class cricket.

title: NEET aspirant jumps to death from Faridabad highrise after calculating probable score
content: Tragedy struck in Faridabad as an 18-year-old NEET aspirant took her own life on Thursday. After reviewing her probable score, which she calculated to be approximately 600 out of 720, she allegedly leapt from the 16th floor. Distressed over her potential results, family members noted concerns leading up to the tragic event, prompting an ongoing police investigation into the circumstances surrounding her death.

title: Delimitation debate: How opposition faultlines may help govt in Monsoon session
content: There are strong speculations that the government is planning to reintroduce the Delimitation Bill during upcoming Monsoon Session. The Bill could not pass the numbers test during the Budget session. Opposition parties, which were united in their opposition to the Bill in April, appear to be divided now over the idea of uniform seat increase.  Some parties may support the bill if their concerns are addressed.  The Congress fears isolation and has requested an all-party meeting for discussions.  The proposed 50% seat increase aims to facilitate women's reservation without altering current proportions.

title: US may add $100,000 bond to Green Card applicants seeking permanent residence
content: The US government is considering a $100,000 bond for Green Card applicants. This measure aims to ensure immigrants are financially self-sufficient and do not burden public programs. The Trump administration is exploring this long-standing legal authority under the Immigration and Nationality Act. This proposal follows earlier attempts to impose fees on H-1B visa applications. Officials stated this policy reduced overstays in pilot programs.

title: FIFA World Cup final frenzy: Maharashtra hotels push to keep bars open till 3.30am
content: Maharashtra's hospitality industry seeks extended bar operating hours for the FIFA World Cup Final.  The association requested permission to remain open until 3:30 AM on July 20.  This move aims to provide a safe environment for fans watching the global sporting event.  Other states have granted similar one-time relaxations during past World Cup finals.  The government's decision will impact fan arrangements and the hospitality sector's business.

title: Winners will make the hard choices: Microsoft's security chief to employees after layoffs
content: Microsoft's new security chief Hayete Gallot has replaced senior executives, merged engineering teams, and cut hundreds of jobs to push the company toward AI-powered cybersecurity, The Information reports. In an internal memo, Gallot told staff the industry is being reimagined and rewards firms that make hard choices early. Microsoft is chasing AI security spending flowing to rivals OpenAI and Anthropic.

title: Human voices near salmon streams made bears and eagles flee, study finds
content: New findings from Canadian researchers reveal that human sounds near Alaska's salmon streams significantly disturb wildlife, leading to grizzly bears avoiding feeding areas. This interference disrupts the natural nutrient transfer to surrounding forests, emphasizing the detrimental effects of human activity on essential ecosystem dynamics. Scientists propose enhanced land management practices to safeguard these critical ecological processes.

title: While ships struggled, billionaire’s $300m superyacht crossed Hormuz
content: Motor Yacht A, under the ownership of billionaire Andrey Melnichenko, recently traversed the Strait of Hormuz amidst increased military tensions that have prompted commercial vessels to postpone their voyages. Following the imposition of Western sanctions after Russia's invasion of Ukraine, Melnichenko relocated his assets to the Gulf, particularly after his Sailing Yacht A was confiscated by Italian authorities in March 2022.

title: Xi Jinping challenges American supremacy after US-led AI safety agency push
content: Chinese President Xi Jinping presented a new global artificial intelligence vision.  He promoted China's open-source technology as a worldwide public good.  This initiative directly counters the US-led alliance and its safety agency proposals.  China also launched the World AI Cooperation Organisation for global tech inclusion.  Beijing aims to share its AI breakthroughs and expertise with developing nations.

title: FIFA WC final in danger? Wildfire haze engulfs New Jersey as Spain train through smoke
content: Wildfire smoke has created unhealthy air quality across New York and New Jersey.  The FIFA World Cup final between Argentina and Spain is scheduled for Sunday.  Organisers are monitoring conditions closely for potential weather disruptions.  Spain trained outdoors despite hazardous air quality levels on Thursday.  Forecasts suggest improving conditions and rainfall may clear the smoke before the match.

title: Apple overtakes Nvidia to become world's most valuable company again
content: Apple Inc. has regained its position as the world's most valuable company. Nvidia's stock experienced a sharp decline, reducing its market capitalization significantly. Investors are now favoring companies with strong long-term growth prospects over AI boom beneficiaries. Apple's recent share surge and China's approval for Apple Intelligence support its valuation. HSBC upgraded Apple's stock, citing an improving operational outlook for the company.

title: 'Colonial mindset endures': Mehbooba slams Centre over silence on Wangchuk's fast
content: PDP chief Mehbooba Mufti criticized the government's silence on Sonam Wangchuk's hunger strike.  Wangchuk has been on an indefinite hunger strike for twenty days demanding action.  He is protesting alleged irregularities in the NEET examination and paper leaks.  Doctors have warned of critical health risks if the hunger strike continues.  Wangchuk vows to continue his protest until July twentieth for a government response.

title: Connor McDavid trade rumor: Flyers emerge as potential landing spot for Oilers star
content: Connor McDavid is back in the center of NHL trade rumors, this time because former NHL player Todd Fedoruk thinks the Philadelphia Flyers could be a possible destination. The comments have opened the debate about the long-term future of the Edmonton Oilers captain despite his contract and no-movement clause, even if there are no confirmed trade talks.

title: Erling Haaland's net worth soars after 2026 World Cup as global fame boosts fortune
content: Erling Haaland heads into the post-2026 World Cup period with a net worth of approximately $100 million, backed by $80 million in annual earnings, a $30 million real estate portfolio, and brand deals with Nike, Breitling, and Beats by Dre. Forbes ranks him the fourth highest-paid player at the tournament. The World Cup supercharged his social media presence, with nearly 29 million new Instagram followers in six weeks, pushing his audience past 69 million and raising his estimated earnings per sponsored post to roughly $695,900. At 25, his financial ceiling is still rising.

title: 'Kattar Beimaan Party': PM Modi attacks AAP at Jalandhar rally
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi criticised Punjab's ruling Aam Aadmi Party for corruption allegations. He labeled the party "Kattar Beimaan Party" during a rally in Jalandhar. The prime minister also highlighted the state's deteriorating law and order situation. He mentioned a former AAP leader's conviction for murder during Delhi riots. Modi urged voters to elect the BJP for a prosperous Punjab.

title: Stranger than fiction: Messi, Yamal set for WC final 19 years after first meeting
content: Lionel Messi and Lamine Yamal will face each other in the World Cup final.  Nineteen years ago, Messi bathed baby Yamal during a charity photoshoot event.  Yamal has since become Spain's biggest football star at Barcelona.  Messi left Barcelona in 2021, while Yamal emerged as a global superstar.  This remarkable full-circle moment culminates in Sunday's biggest football match.

title: RIL Q2 FY27: Profit falls 22% after Asian Paints gain; revenue rises 25% YoY
content: RIL results: Revenue from operations rose 25% year-on-year to Rs 3.11 lakh crore in the June quarter, compared with Rs 2.48 lakh crore in the same quarter last year. Quarterly EBITDA increased 10% from a year earlier to Rs 54,067 crore.

title: Hormuz closure: Why crude oil prices haven’t spiralled out of control
content: Brent crude prices in the spot market briefly surged to around $144 per barrel before easing as markets adjusted. Yet as ADB notes in its report - despite this unprecedented supply shock, crude oil prices did not reach levels seen during the 1973 Arab oil embargo or even the 1990 Gulf war, if one were to inflation-adjust the prices.

title: Spanish PM, King to attend World Cup final as Argentine President stays home
content: Spain's Prime Minister and King will attend the World Cup final match.  US President Donald Trump also confirmed his attendance at the New York stadium.  Argentina's President Javier Milei will watch from home due to superstition.  He believes his presence at home brings Argentina luck in matches.  This decision follows a tradition of avoiding presidential attendance at crucial games.

title: CM Vijay inspects adi dravidar hostel in Chennai; students tell him about worms in food
content: Several students preparing for TNPSC and UPSC examinations said the hostel gates were shut by 9.30pm, forcing those returning late from work or coaching classes to sleep outside, including in nearby parks.

title: Oscar award winning actor Brenda Fricker of 'Home Alone 2' fame passes away at 81
content: Brenda Fricker, the acclaimed Irish actor, has passed away at the age of eighty-one. She was the first Irish actor to win an Academy Award for her role. Fricker was widely recognized for her performances in My Left Foot and Home Alone 2. Her career spanned decades across film, television, and theatre productions. The world remembers her for her sincere and emotionally rich acting contributions.

title: 'Go to your MLA': BJP legislator's response to water crisis sparks protest in MP's Shivpuri
content: In an incident highlighting political disconnect, a BJP MLA disregarded the pleas of villagers at a public grievance meeting, insisting they were not his voters. In protest, the villagers placed a child atop the MLA’s vehicle to draw attention to their pleas concerning water and electricity shortages. The striking act of dissent has taken social media by storm, with the protest video circulating widely among users.

title: I went to Israel nervous. I returned with more questions than answers
content: Beyond the war headlines lay coexistence, political argument, cultural mixing and contradictions that no short visit could fully resolve.

title: Why railways picked Jind-Sonipat route for its 1st hydrogen train
content: India has joined select nations operating hydrogen-powered trains with its first service. The Jind-Sonipat route was chosen for its low traffic and infrastructure availability. This 10-coach train is the longest of its kind globally. It utilizes indigenous technology and a powerful fuel-cell propulsion system. The service will eventually be extended to New Delhi.

title: Cocaine & guns: How FIFA's World Cup final referee was caught up in a police raid
content: Slavko Vincic will referee the FIFA World Cup 2026 final between Argentina and Spain.  This prestigious assignment follows a past controversy involving a police raid.  Vincic was questioned as a witness and cleared of any wrongdoing.  He has previously officiated a match involving Argentina in 2022.  His career has continued to flourish after the incident.

title: FIDE Awards shortlists: Gukesh, Praggnanandhaa, Divya lead India's strong showing
content: Indian chess stars Gukesh, Praggnanandhaa, and Divya Deshmukh are nominated for FIDE Excellence Awards.  They will compete against global talent in multiple prestigious player categories.  The All India Chess Federation also received a nomination for Best Federation.  Indian national teams and ChessBase India Foundation earned recognition for their achievements.  Winners will be announced at the Chess Olympiad.

title: Ram Mandir donation case: Tinnu Yadav, Manish Yadav sent to 39-hour police remand
content: Two individuals, Ram Shankar Yadav and Manish Yadav, received a 39-hour police remand on Friday. This remand is connected to the alleged embezzlement of Ayodhya Ram temple donations. Custodial interrogation is crucial for uncovering the conspiracy and tracing the money trail. Earlier, two other accused were also taken for custodial interrogation as part of the ongoing probe. The alleged donation scam has triggered significant political backlash against the ruling BJP.

title: India's U-19 WC-winning star arrested over alleged attempt to bribe player
content: Former India U-19 star Manjot Kalra was arrested in Sri Lanka on Friday.  He faces allegations of attempting to bribe a player during the Lanka Premier League.  Kalra, a co-owner of the Jaffna Kings, was taken into custody by investigators.  The alleged bribery attempt was reported by several Jaffna Kings players.  Sri Lanka Cricket stated it will cooperate with the ongoing investigation.

title: NRI says neighbor called cops when she and her husband were arguing
content: An Indian woman in Texas sought help on social media after her husband was taken by the police based on the complaint of a neighbor who also told the police that the husband frequently shouted at the wife. The wife said when police came they saw minor injury on her as she was pushed during the argument.

title: Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan' undergoes major revamp after online leak ahead of July release
content: Jana Nayagan's release is now set for July 23 after facing censorship and leak challenges.  The producer confirmed significant modifications and additions were made to the film.  These alterations occurred after some footage leaked online weeks before its censor certificate.  The film's title card for Vijay has also reportedly been updated by the makers.  Audiences will experience a very different version of the movie in theatres.

title: Mumbai mayor rejects BMC report on Chembur tree crash that killed 11-year-old
content: Mumbai Mayor Ritu Tawde rejected the BMC inquiry report on the Chembur tree crash.  She directed the civic administration to conduct a fresh investigation into the incident.  Corporators criticized the report for failing to fix accountability for the child's death.  The report imposed only a nominal penalty on the contractor, which was deemed insufficient.  Vihaan Srivastav's father also questioned the inquiry's transparency and accountability.

title: Beware of clicking on 3 dots: You may lose money to cons
content: Cyber experts warn about remote device takeover fraud scams targeting mobile users.  A Lucknow woman lost over three lakh rupees in such a sophisticated scam.  Fraudsters posed as customer support executives to trick the victim's child.  They obtained screen-sharing permissions by asking for prompts to be tapped.  This allowed criminals to access banking apps and steal funds.

title: 'To atone donation theft, seek forgiveness': Ayodhya Ram temple trust begins 10-day purification ritual
content: A 10-day purification ritual has begun in Ayodhya for alleged donation theft.  The Shri Ram Janmabhoomi Teerth Kshetra Trust initiated this special ceremony.  This ritual involves daily recitations of the Vishnu Sahasranama Stotra.  The Valmiki Ramayan is also being recited as part of the purification.  This event follows weeks after the alleged theft triggered political controversy.

title: RITES: Double-decker flyover undercuts Bengaluru Metro gains
content: Engineering consultancy RITES has flagged issues with Bengaluru's proposed double-decker flyover. The report suggests this plan could undermine the Metro's purpose and encourage private vehicle use. Building the structure may also present significant engineering and operational challenges for the city. This proposal runs contrary to national policies promoting sustainable urban mobility. The Karnataka government will now decide the project's future course.

title: 'Harassed by seniors': Teacher drives car into Godavari river, kills self and 2 kids
content: A teacher and his two children died after their car plunged into the Godavari river. Police suspect the tragic incident was a deliberate act of suicide. The teacher had posted a WhatsApp status alleging harassment by his seniors. Local residents alerted authorities, and rescue teams recovered the vehicle and bodies. An investigation is now underway into the allegations and the deaths.

title: Civilian ‘dies of gunshot wounds’ in J&K’s Bhaderwah; Internet suspended in Chenab Valley areas
content: Deceased’s wife joins protests; hundreds of locals assemble in Bhaderwah and condemn “killing of innocent civilian”

title: Centre responds positively to five key cultural proposals, says Minister Kandula Durgesh
content: Circular soon on National School of Drama in Rajamahendravaram, Indian Council for Cultural Relations centre in State, Lalit Kala Akademi, Kalakshetra Auditorium in Vijayawada, and Sahitya Akademi centre in Kadapa, he says

title: How satellite maps are leading fishermen to bigger catches
content: The Potential Fishing Zone advisory, developed by Hyderabad-based INCOIS, has benefited nearly 14 lakh fishermen over past two decades by using satellite data to identify waters where fish are likely to aggregate; the system is now evolving beyond improving catches to promoting sustainable fishing through species-specific advisories and ecological safeguards

title: Naidu seeks Nirmala Sitharaman’s support for setting up of Indian School of Agriculture in A.P.
content: Chief Minister also seeks Central government's intervention to stabilise prices of raw materials used in the manufacture of shrimp feed as steep hike is adversely impacting business

title: Oceansat-3A likely to be launched by year-end, says NRSC Director
content: Coastal observation research laboratory inaugurated at Andhra University’s Centre for Studies on Bay of Bengal

title: Panel defers adoption of report on Bill for removal of PMs, CMs after 30 days in jail
content: Committee pauses proceedings after voting on two recommendations; members seek further consultations on legislation proposing automatic removal of public functionaries held in judicial custody

title: Another case of minor girl getting pregnant comes to light
content: Close on the heels of a minor girl delivering a baby, another case of minor girl getting pregnant because of alleged sexual exploitation has come to light in Dharwad district.

title: Why 20 lakh students excluded from Thalliki Vandanam, asks APCC chief Sharmila
content: Implementation of the scheme mirrors the shortcomings of Amma Vodi programme of the previous YSRCP government, she says in a social media post

title: Five dead after train collides with car in West Bengal’s Murshidabad; 4 children among deceased
content: Railway gateman arrested; CM Suvendu Adhikari offers condolences; Opposition raises systematic failure issue; Congress leader Adhir Ranjan Chowdhury visited the accident site

title: Probe into recurring signal failures holds local technician responsible
content: Whistleblower motorman, who stopped his train on seeing danger ahead, held “blameworthy” for not following rules

title: Experts call for responsible AI at VIT-AP conference
content: Industry leaders and researchers highlight ethical innovation, real-world applications and collaboration to advance Artificial Intelligence research

title: Madras High Court dismisses plea for CBI probe into the alleged bid to bribe TVK MLA
content: A Bench of Chief Justice Sushrut Arvind Dharmadhikari and Justice G. Arul Murugan has said a court of law will not transfer a statutory investigation from one agency to another solely on the basis of newspaper headlines or viral video/audio clips circulating on digital platforms regarding the alleged ‘horse trading’ complaints

title: Kerala’s traditional architecture has always been environmentally sustainable, says Tourism Minister
content: P.C. Vishnunadh inaugurates 9th edition of ‘The Hindu Home Expo’ in Thiruvananthapuram; three-day event features more than 150 properties from 40 leading builders, priced between ₹55 lakh and ₹5 crore

title: Naidu lists issues to be raised in monsoon session of Parliament
content: He tells TDP MPs to highlight, among others, the financial support the State expects from the Centre and the progress of the Amaravati and  Polavaram irrigation project works

title: Kerala power cuts: Pinarayi Vijayan accuses UDF govt of poor planning
content: Kerala’s Leader of the Opposition says LDF government was able to avoid such situations in the past decade by taking necessary steps in advance

title: Talrop director board member arrested in investment fraud case
content: The accused, a native of Pangode, was apprehended on the basis of a complaint filed by a couple from Kallara, who alleged that they were defrauded of ₹10 lakh after investing in the Kochi-based company

title: Yediyurappa joins BJP protest against Bidadi Township Project; party to launch march from Bidadi to Bengaluru
content: Yediyurappa lashes out at the government, alleging that despite the severe drought in the State, it has failed to respond to people’s hardships and was instead trying to acquire fertile farmland

title: IT employees protest CorroHealth layoffs, demand stronger regulations for the sector
content: They say the labour codes introduced by the Union government do not provide effective penalties against companies that violate labour laws

title: Kerala CM refuses to meet MLA, ignores KSU president at public event
content: Alappuzha MLA A.D. Thomas had demanded action against ADGP M.R. Ajith Kumar, while KSU president Aloshious Xavier had taken strong exception to the appointment of government pleaders citing their alleged links with the SFI in the past

title: Harjit Singh Grewal appointed National Commission for Minorities chief
content: With the appointment of the Chairperson, the Vice-Chairperson and a member, the Commission now has four members against its sanctioned strength of seven

title: BMRCL to implement new metro penalty rules; fines increased up to ₹25,000 under centre’s notification
content: According to officials, the move is part of the Centre’s broader Jan Vishwas initiative to promote trust-based governance and simplify the enforcement of laws

title: El Niño threatens kharif sowing in Chittoor and Annamayya districts
content: Weather reports from the Central and State agencies indicate a high probability that El Niño conditions will persist through the remainder of 2026, raising fears of below-normal rainfall, prolonged dry spells, and rising temperatures

title: Cashew import scam: Kerala High Court directs Secretary to submit prosecution sanction order with signature
content: The Central Bureau of Investigation had said that the absence of signature could cause impediments to the probe

title: Women Safety Wing trains educators to lead ‘Stand with Her’ campaign across Telangana
content: The training featured interactive sessions on gender sensitivity, identifying inappropriate behaviour, bystander intervention, facilitation techniques and practical strategies

title: Fervour marks annual ‘Ani Vara Asthanam’ at Tirumala
content: The festival concluded with Lord Malayappa Swamy and His consorts being taken out in a grand procession on ‘Pushpa Pallaki’ along the Mada Streets

title: Sitaphalmandi-Lallaguda 3-km rail chord line gives trains a faster route, eases pressure on Secunderabad station
content: The Railways completed key infrastructure works on the stretch, including five minor bridges, a major road-over-bridge at the Alugadda Bhavi junction between Rail Nilayam and Mettuguda Road

title: Centre launches scheme for employers to settle provident fund cases
content: Government says objective of the scheme is to promote voluntary compliance, reduce litigation, and enable speedy resolution of long-pending disputes relating to penalty/damages while safeguarding the interests of employees

title: DDMA had warned of July 7 debris slip in Wayanad, amicus curiae tells Kerala High Court
content: It says that the District Disaster Management Authority had directed the executing agencies that the excavated earth near the Anakkampoyil–Kalladi–Meppaditwin tube tunnel road project site be removed, to avert a disaster

title: Kanaka Durga temple officials push to complete key works before Shravana Masam
content: Master plan envisages a multi-level parking complex and a second ghat road to accommodate rising pilgrim footfall, improve traffic management and enhance safety at the hill shrine

title: Minor's Parents "Sell" Her Baby After Rape, She Is Pregnant Again
content: According to police, the minor was lured and sexually assaulted on multiple occasions in 2025, resulting in pregnancy.

title: Internet Suspended In J&K's Doda After Man Killed, 2 Injured In Shooting
content: Internet services were suspended in Jammu and Kashmir's Doda district after a civilian was killed and two others were injured in a shooting incident, officials said.

title: Pakistan Army Kills 24 Taliban, Baloch Militants During Raids, Weapons Seized
content: The military said Friday it used intelligence sources to target militants in operations carried out over the previous day that killed 24 members of the outlawed Pakistani Taliban and Baloch separatist groups.

title: Maharashtra State Transport Bus Fares To Be Hiked By 13.5% From Tomorrow
content: MSRTC is one of the largest public bus service corporations in the country with a fleet of more than 14,000 buses. Daily more than 55 lakh passengers use its bus service.

title: "Jinnah Worshippers": Yogi Adityanath's Swipe At Congress, Samajwadi Party
content: Yogi Adityanath was in Shamli district for the inauguration and foundation-laying ceremony for 89 development projects worth over Rs 581 crore.

title: SpiceJet Fined Rs 1 Lakh By Consumer Protection Body Over 'Dark Patterns'
content: The CCPA said the authority had found that such practices "impair consumer autonomy, undermine informed decision-making and are inconsistent with the principles of fair and transparent consumer engagement".

title: Murshidabad Train Accident Updates: 3 Students Among 4 Killed In Train-Van Collision In West Bengal's Murshidabad
content: Murshidabad Train Accident Live Updates: The incident reportedly occured at 7 am, when students were headed to the school.

title: Sub-Inspector's Body Found With Blood On Clothes Inside Parked Car In Lucknow
content: Based on an identity card found in his pocket during the search, the man was identified as Sub-Inspector Ajay Thakur, posted at the Ghazipur police station.

title: Court Junks FIR Against Actor Shraddha Kapoor's Brother In 2022 Drug Case
content: The case dates back to June 2022, when Bengaluru Police detained Siddhanth Kapoor and a few others during a raid at a party in the city over allegations of drug consumption.

title: Apple Pips Nvidia As World's Biggest Company As Chipmaker's Shares Dip 3.7%
content: Nvidia shares sank 3.7 per cent on Friday to bring the chipmaker's market capitalisation down to $4.8 trillion, while Apple's stock climbed 0.4 per cent, putting its valuation at $4.9 trillion.

title: CBI Files 2nd Chargesheet In Reliance Communications Loan Fraud Case
content: The agency had registered the case on the basis of a complaint filed by the State Bank of India (SBI).

title: In PM Modi's Punjab Speech, References To Drugs And Tahir Hussain
content: He claimed the Centre had sent large sums of money to Punjab over the years, but the funds were being misused.

title: Kuwait Says Army Personnel Injured In Iranian Drone Attack
content: The army's chief of staff "visited several wounded members of the Kuwaiti land forces", it said in a press statement.

title: Iran Strikes US Airbase In Qatar "To Punish The Aggressor"
content: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said Friday that they had targeted US radar systems and military aircraft in Qatar.

title: 'No Hatred': Australia Vows Crackdown On Racism Against Indian Truckers
content: Asked about a rise in racial slurs, verbal harassment and even death threats delivered over CB radio to Indian-origin drivers, Burke acknowledged the trend as a "grim reality" and laid out a two-part response: what the government is doing and what it is saying, insisting both matter equally.

title: On Camera, Elderly Man Slapped By Auto Driver After Argument In Bengaluru
content: An elderly man was slapped by an autorickshaw driver following a heated argument on a Bengaluru road, with the entire incident captured on a nearby car's dashboard camera.

title: Congress To Join Omar Abdullah's Protest For Jammu And Kashmir's Statehood
content: Tariq Hamid Karra, the J&K Congress President, said the party's central leadership will also join the protests on July 20.

title: Hotter Nights Stealing Sleep Across World, India Among Hardest Hit
content: Cities across the Middle East recorded the highest heat-related sleep losses, while parts of South Asia, Southeast Asia and West Africa also showed significant impacts.

title: Skyroot's Vikram-1 To Carry Micro-Sculptures Of Indian Icons Into Space
content: If the mission succeeds, Vikram-1 could become the first Indian rocket to carry micro art into space, creating a unique fusion of art, science and space exploration.

title: 'Whoever kills Trump ...': Iran-backed Iraqi militia puts $10m bounty on US prez
content: Kanchan Yadav is a writer at The Times of India, where she covers business, politics, international affairs and social issues. Her reporting also focuses on policy, governance and economic developments, with a close lens on how decisions in corridors of power affect everyday lives and community well-being. Read More

content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More

title: Andy Burnham set to be new UK PM, will take over from Keir Starmer on Monday
content: he was the only contender in the leadership contest to replace departing Prime Minister Keir Starmer . he secured nominations from 379 of the 403 Labour lawmakers in the house of commons . the former mayor of Greater Manchester has been prime minister-in-waiting for weeks .

title: G.D.N Release Date: R Madhavan-led biopic to hit theatres on August 7; Netflix run to follow
content: The TOI Entertainment Desk is a dynamic and dedicated team of journalists, working tirelessly to bring the pulse of the entertainment world straight to the readers of The Times of India. No red carpet goes unrolled, no stage goes dark - our team spans the globe, bringing you the latest scoops and insider insights from Bollywood to Hollywood, and every entertainment hotspot in between. We don't just report; we tell tales of stardom and stories untold. Whether it's the rise of a new sensation or the seasoned journey of an industry veteran, the TOI Entertainment Desk is your front-row seat to the fascinating narratives that shape the entertainment landscape. Beyond the breaking news, we present a celebration of culture. We explore the intersections of entertainment with society, politics, and everyday life. Read More

title: Consumer panel fines SpiceJet for ‘deploying dark patterns on its booking platform’
content: the airline has been directed to permanently discontinue default-consent practices . the violations include: 'automatic enrolment into SpiceClub. Pre-selected consent for promotional communications. Deceptive interface design influencing consumer decisions'

title: Pakistan's fifth province? Gilgit-Baltistan assembly passes resolution amid PoK unrest
content: Abhishek Mishra is a Digital Content Producer at The Times of India, working with the news desk. He writes with a keen eye on foreign policy, defence developments, and the shifting currents of geopolitics-and yes, Indian politics always finds a way onto his radar too. Read More

title: Skyroot’s rocket Vikram-1 will carry to space a special message from PM Modi
content: skyroot's Vikram-1 will fly to space at 11.30am on saturday . it will carry a message for the universe from Prime Minister Narendra Modi . the handwritten card has PM Modi’s signature and has the date 26-06-26 written in his writing .

title: ‘Just like 'Gandhi' film not based on Mahatma’: Aamir Khan faces political backlash for remarks
content: aamir Khan's remarks on Sonam Wangchuk sparks political backlash . he says neither he nor director knew him when they made '3 idiots' . political parties accuse him of being unwilling to take a stand out of fear of the ruling establishment .

title: 'Who is D nexT': Iran billboard targets Trump after Graham's death
content: the billboard features the name of recently deceased US Senator Lindsey Graham . it features what appears to be a hidden reference to assasination threats for president . conservative voices in Iran have portrayed Graham's death as divine punishment .

title: 'Individual opinion': India rejects ex-Japan minister's claims on bullet train delays
content: Trisha Mahajan is a journalist with The Times of India digital, with keen interest in covering business and corporate affairs. She also reports on key national and world developments and global news trends. A keen reader with a strong interest in storytelling that adds context, clarity, and depth to complex issues.
